question: When is the best time to apply moisturiser?

Whenever you remember during the day

To make your skin produce less oil

question: Which season typically makes skin feel the driest?

question: What is the difference between a lotion and a cream moisturiser?
options: Lotions are thinner with more water, creams are thicker
Creams are always better for all skin types
Lotions are only for body, never face

question: What does an occlusant in a moisturiser do?

question: Which is an example of an occlusant ingredient?

options: Yes, all lotions work the same
No, body lotions can be too heavy and may clog facial pores
Only if you run out of face moisturiser permanently

question: What is a sleeping mask in skincare?
options: A mask you wear to block out light while sleeping
A thick overnight moisturiser you leave on while you sleep
A product to reduce dark circles
A cooling gel for puffy eyes

question: How can you tell if your skin is dehydrated rather than just dry?
options: Dehydrated skin may feel tight and look dull but produce oil too
Dry and dehydrated skin are always the same
Dehydrated skin only happens in summer

question: What food group is great for skin hydration from the inside out?
options: Fruits and vegetables with high water content

question: Which of these fruits is great for skin hydration because of its high water content?

options: No, you already applied it in the morning
Yes, reapplying can help relieve tightness and dryness
Only if you washed your face again

question: What does ceramide in a moisturiser do for your skin?
options: Helps rebuild and strengthen the skin barrier

question: What is the skin barrier and why does it matter?
options: A layer that makes skin shiny
The outermost layer that protects skin and locks in moisture
The part of skin that produces oil

question: What can damage your skin barrier and lead to dryness?

options: Using a humidifier in dry or air-conditioned rooms
Washing your face 5 times a day

question: What is a serum and how does it work with moisturiser?
options: A serum is applied after moisturiser
A serum is a lightweight concentrated treatment applied before moisturiser
A serum is the same as a toner

question: If you have combination skin, where do you typically need more moisturiser?

question: What is a toner and how does it help moisturiser work better?

A toner replaces moisturiser for oily skin
A toner is only used to remove makeup

question: Can eating a lot of sugar affect your skin hydration?
options: No, sugar has no effect on skin
Yes, too much sugar can damage collagen and contribute to dull dry skin

question: What is the best way to apply moisturiser to your face?

Spray it on from a distance

question: What does it mean if a moisturiser says it has peptides?

question: Which of these is a sign that your current moisturiser may not be right for your skin?

question: What happens to skin hydration as we get older?

Age has no impact on skin hydration
Older skin always looks dewy and plump naturally

options: At night, as retinol can make skin sensitive to sunlight
